The problem involves a string of nodes (n1 to n8) that needs to be rotated.
The domain defines a rotation process involving two passes.
1. `rotate`: Changes the heading of the pivot node `n` and sets up the first pass cursor.
2. `rotate-first-pass`: Iterates through the string, updating headings and clearing old positions.
3. `rotate-first-pass-end`: Finishes the first pass and sets up the second pass cursor.
4. `rotate-second-pass`: Iterates through the string, setting new positions based on the new headings.
5. `rotate-second-pass-end`: Finishes the second pass and clears the `rotating` flag.
